Question: Use the midpoint rule with $$n = 2$$ to estimate $$\int_{1}^{2} \frac{1}{x} d x .$$
Use the trapezoidal rule with $$n = 2$$ to estimate $$\int_{1}^{2} \frac{1}{x} d x .$$

In an earlier checkpoint, we estimated $$\int_{1}^{2} \frac{1}{x} d x$$ to be $$\frac{24}{35}$$ using $$T_{2} .$$ The actual value of this integral is $$\text{ln} 2 .$$ Using $$\frac{24}{35} \approx 0.6857$$ and $$\text{ln} 2 \approx 0.6931 ,$$ calculate the absolute error and the relative error.
Use Equation 3.13 to find an upper bound for the error in using $$M_{4}$$ to estimate $$\int_{0}^{1} x^{2} d x .$$
